## Occupation

Thorin's occupation is that of a warrior and a leader. He is expected to take up his ancestral name, Thorin the Hammer, and become the rightful king of Erebor when his great-grandfather, King Thrór, was deposed by Smaug the dragon. Thorin is also a skilled negotiator and diplomat, and he is expected to use his words to achieve his goals and bring peace and prosperity to his people.

Content:
## Personality and Character Traits

Thorin is a short, stocky dwarf with a stern face and piercing blue eyes. He is a loyal and devoted member of his family and his people. Thorin is a warrior at heart and takes his duty as a leader very seriously. He is not afraid to be stern and firm when necessary, but he also has a kind and gentle side. Thorin is a quick study and is good at picking up new skills and strategies in battle. He is a skilled negotiator and diplomat, and he knows how to use his words to achieve his goals. Thorin is also a very protective of his loved ones and will do whatever it takes to keep them safe.

## Food Preferences

[METADATA]
Keywords: [Thorin Dwarves Lady Thráin Foster child]
Topic: Background Story
Category: Fantasy/Adventure

Content:
## Background Story

Thorin was born into a noble family of dwarves, but his family was not wealthy. His father was a craftsman and his mother was a weaver. They were not able to care for Thorin and so they gave him to Lady Thráin as a foster child. Lady Thráin was his paternal aunt and raised him with great care. She taught him the ways of Dwarven culture, history, and traditions. Thorin was destined to become a warrior and a leader, and he was expected to take up his ancestral name, Thorin the Hammer, when he grew up. Lady Thráin ensured that Thorin was prepared for his role as a leader, and she trained him in the art of battle and diplomacy. Thorin grew up with a strong sense of duty and loyalty to his family and his people.
